03 Stop Annual Planning - YouTube.mp4
Before you begin architecting your company's meeting rhythm, there is a massive mindset shift that you're gonna have to make. And that is, you need to stop doing annual planning. Yep, you heard what I said. I want you to stop doing annual planning. Now, I realize this will sound crazy to most entrepreneurs because everyone says that you need to do annual planning, right? But here's the deal. Annual plans suck, okay? They are too long to be predictable, but too short to do something truly meaningful, so they're simultaneously unhelpful and unmotivating. And yet, everyone does them. Why? I mean, think about all the entrepreneurs who spent weeks, even months, planning for 2020, only to have all those precious plans dashed when a pandemic shut down the economy. entire world.
Now that's an extreme example, but it kind of speaks to the problem with annual plans. 12 months is just too much future to predict with any degree of accuracy. Sure, the massive Fortune 500 companies with entire floors filled with analysts can probably predict what will happen over the next year with, I don't know, some degree of accuracy, but... I can't, and your average bootstrapped company can't either. I mean, can you? But at the same time, 12 months isn't enough future to ride out the inevitable bumps and bruises that the entrepreneurial rollercoaster throws at you. Nor is it enough time to do something truly meaningful that can make a real impact and create a dent in your little corner. corner of the universe. The fact is, it's just an awkward amount of time, so we ignore it as a unit of measurement, and I would encourage you to do the same. Once you move beyond the annual planning mindset, you are free to implement the scalable planning system. So, let's get to that now.
